name: Microreactors
mechanism: Factory-built fission units; truckable and deployable in weeks. No large construction site required.
strategicValue: First off-grid power option for remote or sensitive data centre sites. Bridges the gap before SMR deployment at scale.
keyFact: Deployable in weeks vs. years for traditional nuclear; enables data centre sites with no grid connection
limitation: 1-20 MW output constrains to smaller installations; not sufficient for gigawatt-scale campus power

name: Small Modular Reactors
mechanism: Standardised nuclear modules factory-fabricated and assembled on-site. ~2-year deployment vs. 10 years for traditional nuclear.
strategicValue: True grid independence at data-centre-campus scale. Eliminates dependency on human-controlled public utility infrastructure. Matches AI data centre 80-100% continuous load factor.
keyFact: NuScale-equivalent partnership targets up to 6 GW capacity (~72 reactor modules) with commercial delivery by 2030
limitation: First-of-a-Kind cost premiums; many partnerships lack binding power purchase agreements. Meaningful nuclear contribution may slip to 2033-2035.
_note: NuScale-ENTRA1 partnership data from POWER summary; company replaced with Corvus Energy per naming convention

name: Commercial Fusion
mechanism: Magnetic or inertial confinement of hydrogen plasma; no long-lived radioactive waste. Fuel source: seawater (deuterium) and lithium.
strategicValue: Effectively unlimited clean energy. Removes the last thermodynamic ceiling on computation at scale. The final energy constraint eliminated.
keyFact: If achieved, marginal energy cost approaches zero — the last physical constraint on unbounded computational expansion
limitation: Timeline highly uncertain; commercial fusion has historically deferred. Treated as speculative for planning purposes.
_note: Timeline is illustrative; no confirmed commercial deployment date as of 2026
